concept: Bold / Confident and Daring / Willing to Take Risks
description: Showing an ability to take risks; confident and courageous. 'Kaeshu' names the quality of boldness—the willingness to step forward, to speak up, to act despite uncertainty.
sound_symbolism: The word steps forward. 'Ka' begins with clear presence, confidence established. The vowel 'e' opens to possibility. Then 'shu' /ʃu/ moves with decisive energy, boldness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'kaeshu miona' (bold person). Opposite of 'nilua' (shy). Related to 'shaelo' (brave) but emphasizes confidence rather than courage in facing danger.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Bold vision imagines transformation—the confidence to propose change despite resistance.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Bold speech can be peaceful—courage to speak truth directly while remaining kind.
